question.text: What accounting guidance does ICAI provide for derivative transactions in India?

answer.text: ICAI 2021 Guidance Note on Accounting for Derivative Contracts — it covers recognition, measurement, hedge accounting, and disclosure requirements for derivatives
ICAI has not issued any guidance on derivative accounting — companies follow their own policies
Only Ind AS 109 applies — ICAI has not issued separate guidance for derivatives
ICAI guidance applies only to banks — other entities follow SEBI accounting standards

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The Institute of Chartered Accountants of India (ICAI) issued the 2021 Guidance Note on Accounting for Derivative Contracts. It covers: initial recognition at fair value, subsequent measurement, hedge accounting requirements (fair value hedge, cash flow hedge, net investment hedge), and comprehensive disclosure. **The Trap:** Ind AS 109 (Financial Instruments) is the standard, but ICAI issued ADDITIONAL guidance specific to derivative contracts. Both apply. The guidance is for ALL entities, not just banks. **Cross-Exam Context:** All three series (I Ch9, IV Ch9, VIII Ch9) reference the ICAI guidance. It's universal across derivative types. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Three types of hedge accounting under ICAI/Ind AS 109: (1) Fair value hedge, (2) Cash flow hedge, (3) Net investment hedge. Each has specific documentation requirements.
